profile.name: Bruno Salgado
profile.role: Software Engineering Tech Lead / Senior Backend Engineer

hero.headline: Tech Lead and Senior Backend Engineer focused on Java, Spring Boot, AWS, payments, and distributed systems.
hero.supportingText: Bruno Salgado builds and leads backend systems where reliability, integration complexity, and delivery discipline matter: payment flows, regulated APIs, cloud modernization, and service architectures that teams can operate with confidence.
hero.availability: Open to Senior Backend Engineer and Tech Lead opportunities in Canada, especially across fintech, backend platform, SaaS, and distributed systems teams.
hero.subheadline: Nearly 8 years across Brazilian banking, US fintech, backend modernization, and provider-heavy platforms.

recruiterSignals.title: Banking and fintech track record
recruiterSignals.body: Experience across Itau Unibanco, PROFIT Business Bank, and Cloud Payments, covering regulated banking systems, financial reporting, partner-heavy fintech platforms, and current Tech Lead scope.

recruiterSignals.title: AWS modernization depth
recruiterSignals.body: Hands-on delivery across AWS and container platforms, including CloudFormation, CodePipeline, Kubernetes, Docker, and the practical work of moving systems away from older on-prem operating models.

recruiterSignals.title: Payments and partner integrations
recruiterSignals.body: Built Java and Spring Boot services for banking, crypto, and card-provider integrations, with strong attention to data accuracy, resilience, and operational clarity.

recruiterSignals.title: Tech lead execution
recruiterSignals.body: Currently leading technical execution across parallel initiatives, validating architecture decisions, surfacing risks early, and helping teams deliver under real timeline pressure.

intro: I grew up technically in regulated banking and then spent the last several years in fintech and payments. That combination shaped how I think: correctness first, clear service boundaries, and enough operational visibility that people can trust the system when something goes wrong.

At Itau Unibanco, I worked on backend services, regulatory tooling, and modernization work in a large banking environment. Later, in US fintech roles, I moved deeper into Java and Spring Boot APIs, provider integrations, and the delivery realities of platforms that have to balance speed, compliance, and reliability.

Today I work as a Software Engineering Tech Lead, which means I still care about code and system design, but I also spend time validating architecture, reducing delivery risk, and helping teams move through complex work without losing clarity.

philosophy.title: Make the system explain itself
philosophy.body: If support, finance, and engineers cannot reconstruct what happened, the design is incomplete. Good backend work makes state, failure paths, and ownership easier to understand.

philosophy.title: Tie architecture to delivery
philosophy.body: Design only becomes valuable when it helps a team execute. I like architecture that shortens ambiguity, improves rollout safety, and gives people clearer decision boundaries.

philosophy.title: Be pragmatic about modernization
philosophy.body: Moving from legacy or on-prem patterns to cloud platforms is rarely a clean rewrite story. The real work is sequencing change safely while still improving the platform underneath.

strengths.title: Payments and financial backends
strengths.body: Java and Spring Boot services for regulated environments, provider integrations, financial reporting, transaction flows, and correctness-sensitive APIs.

strengths.title: AWS and platform execution
strengths.body: Comfortable with AWS, CloudFormation, CodePipeline, Kubernetes, Docker, OpenShift, and the operational details that determine whether a service is genuinely production-ready.

strengths.title: Technical leadership in delivery
strengths.body: Strong in design review, risk identification, execution discipline, and helping teams make architecture decisions that hold up under deadline pressure.

leadership: My leadership style is practical. I like design reviews that actually help, trade-off conversations that stay concrete, and technical direction that reduces churn for the team.

That means validating architecture against delivery reality, being a senior escalation point when complexity spikes, and keeping the system understandable enough that engineers can move without guessing.
I care about reliability and maintainability, but I also care about pace. The best backend leadership balances technical quality with momentum.

intro: Bruno's experience moves from Brazilian banking and regulatory systems into US fintech, cloud payments, and current Tech Lead responsibility.

That progression matters because it combines regulated-system discipline, provider-heavy integrations, AWS modernization work, and the communication needed to keep backend delivery clear as systems scale.

roles.title: Cloud Payments
roles.subtitle: Software Engineering Tech Lead · EU/UK and USA
roles.summary: Leading technical execution across parallel payments initiatives, validating architecture, clarifying ownership boundaries, and reducing delivery risk in systems that depend on reliable provider integrations and scalable backend services.
outcomes: Aligned architecture, sequencing, and service ownership across concurrent initiatives so teams could move faster with less ambiguity.
Strengthened design reviews around scalability, maintainability, and operational visibility before changes reached production payment flows.
Served as a senior escalation point for complex technical issues, helping teams resolve risk earlier and keep delivery steadier under pressure.

roles.title: Cloud Payments
roles.subtitle: Senior Backend Engineer / Java Developer · USA
roles.summary: Built Java and Spring Boot services for a fintech platform integrating with banking, crypto, and card providers, with emphasis on reliability, clean integration boundaries, and controlled delivery in Kubernetes-based environments.
outcomes: Designed and implemented provider-facing services and integration workflows for partners including Circle, Fireblocks, and Ibanera.
Improved resilience around external-provider behavior by challenging failure paths, tightening error handling, and reinforcing operational visibility before release.
Supported scalable delivery through code review, automated testing, and CI/CD discipline across containerized backend services.

roles.title: PROFIT Business Bank
roles.subtitle: Java Spring Developer · USA
roles.summary: Delivered backend services for business-banking reporting workflows where correctness, maintainability, and trustworthy financial output were more important than speed alone.
outcomes: Built backend capabilities for financial reporting features, including cash flow statement workflows for business customers.
Implemented APIs and asynchronous processing paths with careful handling of data consistency and downstream processing boundaries.
Contributed to a banking domain where clearer backend ownership and reliable outputs directly supported operational trust.

roles.title: Itaú Unibanco
roles.subtitle: Software Engineer · Brazil
roles.summary: Worked in a large regulated banking environment on backend services, REST APIs, market-risk tooling, and modernization efforts spanning both cloud and on-prem delivery pipelines.
outcomes: Built backend services and REST APIs in a compliance-heavy environment where traceability, reliability, and maintainability all mattered.
Supported cloud and on-prem CI/CD flows during modernization work, helping teams move legacy workloads toward more scalable operating models.
Contributed to risk and regulatory tooling that required careful change management, stronger operational visibility, and dependable delivery practices.

roles.title: Itaú Unibanco
roles.subtitle: Software Engineering Intern · Brazil
roles.summary: Started in banking software through risk and regulatory work, building the foundations in data, backend logic, and disciplined engineering that shaped the rest of Bruno's career.
outcomes: Helped build a C# system for Brazilian Central Bank risk metrics tied to a formal regulatory demand.
Worked on SQL optimization across queries, procedures, and functions, improving the maintainability of reporting-oriented data flows.
Built internal automations in Visual Basic and developed the discipline required in a regulated engineering environment.

skills.name: Backend Engineering
skills.description: Core delivery stack for business-critical services and APIs.

skills.name: AWS and Platform
skills.description: Cloud and delivery tooling used in real production environments.

skills.name: Architecture and Distributed Systems
skills.description: Design capability that spans service boundaries, failure handling, and long-term maintainability.

skills.name: Payments and Financial Systems
skills.description: Domain strengths shaped by banking, payment products, and provider-heavy fintech work.

skills.name: Leadership and Delivery
skills.description: Hands-on senior execution combined with architecture and team guidance.

contact.heading: Open to Senior Backend Engineer and Tech Lead opportunities in Canada across fintech, payments, backend platform, and distributed systems teams.
contact.summary: If you are hiring for Java, Spring Boot, AWS modernization, payment infrastructure, or backend platform work, Bruno is especially interested in teams where hands-on engineering depth and technical leadership both matter.
contact.responseNote: Use LinkedIn, GitHub, or email directly, or open a pre-filled email draft from the form below for a quick first conversation.
